title: Paronychia gomerensis

The classification of Paronychia gomerensis places it within the genus Paronychia. This genus is part of the family Portulacaceae, which comprises various succulent plants and herbs. Further classification groups Paronychia gomerensis as a member of the order Caryophyllales.

intro: Paronychia gomerensis, also known as Canary Islands spiny plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Nyctaginaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits such as wound healing, skin protection, and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Paronychia gomerensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
